reviews.review_description:
Hi I was looking forward to this since I seen it on rift  but the voice glitches spoiled it please fix it 'coz ilove space
  app's, solor system ect

developer_response.body: can you please let me know what glitches you found and we will push out a patch asap

reviews.review_description: Don't miss this vr experience.
Loved it! Would recommend.

reviews.review_title: Exactly why I bought a VR headset!
reviews.review_description: Games like this are why VR is so incredible. Fantastic experience from start to finish & the one I use to show friends just how great VR can be.
Really enjoyed this.
Won't install

My thanks to the developers.  This experience made me misty eyed on multiple occasions.  VR still has a long way to go but this was obviously made with great care and attention.  I've dreamed of reaching for the stars since, I was a kid.  It was nice to get a taste even if only from my living room!
Such an Immersive experience! Feels so real! If you're into Astronomy then this is a Must buy!! I've personally been interested in Apollo so I was drawn to this automatically!
Extra star for historical accuracy. Otherwise nausea-inducing (and this is coming from a guy who can do 3-axis flips in zero-g games such as Lone Echo or Mission ISS)

This is exactly what I was looking for in an Oculus Go. Very cool and educational, with information about experiments set up by Apollo 11 on the moon, and voice overs consisting of actual audio from the mission. Level of interactivity is ideal for use with the Go controller.
One of the best apps to view first. It will definitely captivate and educate you. A good welcome to the VR world
Although the graphics are not uniformly great, the whole experience feels immersive.  Was there without the white knuckled fear of the real thing, but enough to imagine it.

I really felt like I was there on the mission. Entertaining, emotional, awe inspiring. I’d say it is the best thing I’ve tried on my oculus to date. The historically accurate audio and soundtrack made it extra special.
Fantastic!!!

This is an experience worth having, for sure. However, it's definitely a slow burn. Some parts drag longer than they need to. Still, it is a fun experience, especially with the music and the actual mission recordings. Having done it once, though, I don't feel compelled to go back to it.
As a child my bedroom wall was covered with posters from the Apollo 11 mission and so I loved this experience and listening to some of the classic sound bites.

Can I just say what a fantastic experience I have just had watching Apollo  11.
I remember listening to the moon landing when I was a kid, we were camping. I never dreamt that I could experience the landing myself as I just have. A truly amazing experience on my Oculus Go,  this headset just keeps on giving me every reason to be happy that I bought it.!
A fantastic rendition of a historic period, still vivid in my childhood memories.
Does nobody recall why mission control had a bunch of guys turning blue? Neil Armstrong had to take manual control of the LEM.  This is not depicted.  POS.

reviews.review_title: Houston, we have a great app!
reviews.review_description: I consider the Apollo 11 mission to be the greatest achievement in human history, so I was thrilled to see a VR app devoted to it. It’s obvious that the developers wanted to create not just an immersive experience, but a homage to this historical event. And boy, is it immersive! From every phase of the mission, it’s like you’re right there with Armstrong, Aldrin, and Collins. I do however deduct one star because the control mechanics for the ‘interactive’ story mode are a mess. I ended up just going with the ‘cinema’ mode because I didn’t want to fool with the controls anymore. Nonetheless, it is a breathtaking experience where you feel the vastness of space and come to respect just how unpredictable and dangerous these space missions could potentially be. If you appreciate space exploration and American history, this is a no-brainer.

Sitting on the lauchpad at Kennedy in the command module of Apollo 11 sat atop the mighty Saturn booster and then being hurled into space is a superb VR experience. Everyone in my family who has experienced the launch sequence loved it.

The whole experience takes you from your living room watching Kennedys "We shall go to the moon" speech to the moon landing at Tranquility Base and then back to a fiery re-entry and splashdown in the Pacific. Its all nicely done and you can take control during the docking of the command module with eagle in Earth orbit before departure to the moon and the landing of the eagle lander on the moon. 

Reccomended for anyone with an interest in the history of mans exploration of space.
